popularStory.headline: Murderously awesome

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward III King of England於1312年11月13日出生在英國波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素時，他的父親Edward II King of England28歲，母親Isabella of France Queen of England20歲。他於1328年1月24日與Philippa de Hainault Queen of England在英國英格蘭越可爾約克約克 明斯特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1322年登記服兵役。他於1327年在奧地利提洛爾特利特洛福被列為coronation。他去世於1377年6月21日在英國英格蘭倫敦里士滿享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward III King of England于1312年11月13日出生在英国波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素时，他的父亲Edward II King of England28岁，母亲Isabella of France Queen of England20岁。他于1328年1月24日与Philippa de Hainault Queen of England在英国英格兰越可爾約克約克 明斯特结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1322年登记服兵役。他于1327年在奥地利提洛爾特利特洛福被列为coronation。他去世于1377年6月21日在英国英格兰倫敦里士滿享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward III King of England was born on 13 November 1312, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, his father, Edward II King of England, was 28 and his mother, Isabella of France Queen of England, was 20. He married Philippa de Hainault Queen of England on 24 January 1328, in York Minster, York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. He registered for military service in 1322. In 1327, at the age of 14, his occupation is listed as coronation in Lover, Trient, Tirol, Austria. He died on 21 June 1377, in Richmond, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward III King of England est né le 13 novembre 1312, à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Edward II King of England, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Isabella of France Queen of England, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Philippa de Hainault Queen of England le 24 janvier 1328, à York Minster, York, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1322. En 1327, à l’âge de 14 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est coronation à Lover, Trient, Tirol, Autriche. Il est décédé le 21 juin 1377, à Richmond, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward III King of England am 13. November 1312 in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward II King of England 28 und seine Mutter Isabella of France Queen of England 20. Er hat am 24. Januar 1328 in York Minster, York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Philippa de Hainault Queen of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er hat sich 1322 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1327, als er 14 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als coronation in Lover, Trient, Tirol, Österreich aufgeführt. Er starb am 21. Juni 1377 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Richmond, London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward III King of England è nato il il 13 novembre 1312 nel Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, il suo padre, Edward II King of England, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Isabella of France Queen of England, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Philippa de Hainault Queen of England il 24 gennaio 1328, nel York Minster, York, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1322. Nel 1327, all'età di 14 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come coronation nel Lover, Trentino, Tirolo, Austria. Morì il 21 giugno 1377, nel Richmond, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward III King of Englandが1312年11月13日にイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルで生まれた時，父Edward II King of Englandは28歳，母Isabella of France Queen of Englandは20歳であった。1328年1月24日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークヨーク ミンスターでPhilippa de Hainault Queen of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1322年に兵役登録をした。1327年，14歳の時，オーストリアティロルトリーントロバーでの職業はcoronationと記録されている。彼は1377年6月21日にイギリスイギリスロンドンリチモンドで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward III King of England(이)가 1312년 11월 13일 영국 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward II King of England(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella of France Queen of England(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1328년 1월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 요르크 요르크 민스터에서 Philippa de Hainault Queen of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1322년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1327년, 14세의 나이로 그의 직업은 오스트리아 티럴 트리트 러버에서 coronation이었다. 그는 1377년 6월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 리ch몽에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward III King of England nasceu em 13 de novembro de 1312, em Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward II King of England, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Isabella of France Queen of England, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Philippa de Hainault Queen of England em 24 de janeiro de 1328, em York Minster, York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1322. Em 1327, com 14 anos, sua profissão aparece como coronation em Lover, Trient, Tirol, Áustria. Ele faleceu em 21 de junho de 1377, em Richmond, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward III King of England родился 13 ноября 1312 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward II King of England было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella of France Queen of England, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Philippa de Hainault Queen of England 24 января 1328 г. в местности ерк минстир, ерк, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. В 1322 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1327 году, в возрасте 14 лет, работал по профессии coronation в местности лавир, триент, тирол, Австрия. Он умер 21 июня 1377 г. в местности ричмонд, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward III King of England nació el 13 de noviembre de 1312, en Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward II King of England, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Isabella of France Queen of England, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Philippa de Hainault Queen of England el 24 de enero de 1328, en York Minster, York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1322. En 1327, a la edad de 14 años, su ocupación registrada era coronation en Lover, Trient, Tirol, Austria. Murió el 21 de junio de 1377, en Richmond, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
